fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 64. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 60.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Fully self-supported; optional hostel add-on night before race (May 1); no finish sleep provided.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic self-supported flat loop testing endurance against Dutch winds and distance.

youtube_data.search_query: Race Around Netherlands gran fondo cycling Netherlands (loop)

videos.title: Extreme winds cancel bike race
videos.channel: The Sydney Morning Herald and The Age
videos.description:
Organisers of the Cape Town Cycle Tour call off the event with riders unable to stay on their bikes due to the strong winds.
More - http://www.smh.com.au/sport/cycling/100-kmh-winds-cause-havoc-in-cape-town-cycling-event-20170313-guwroe.html

videos.title: Ronde van Delfzijl 2018 - Finale weekend clubcompetitie - #cycling Holland
videos.description:
The ''Ronde van Delfzijl'' 2018 was a 150km roadrace over a 15km loop. This was the first of 3 races in one weekend. The conditions were really tough, strong wind, cold and rain from about 2 hours into the race. Stay tuned for the VLOG.

Split in the footage at +- 80km, 95km and 150km

The VLOG with summary, tips and commentary HERE: Later this week!

videos.title: 4 HOURS of ATTACKS!! at The DUTCH NATIONAL CHAMPIONSHIPS 2019 Elite Men - Tactics & Power numbers!
videos.description:
The Dutch National Championships Elite Men 2019 Dongen. This race was so tough! 4 hours of non stop attacks! 17x over a 10km lap with sharp corners and 400m cobbles every lap! It was super warm and the competition was high, this made it a really hard race!

Preparing for today: https://youtu.be/Yv1okt4miGE

tags: how to race a road bike
videos.transcript:
what's up guys what's up cycling
fanatics welcome back to another race

board with the national championships
the Dutch national championships in the
a lead man

gonna discuss the tactics and why and

gonna talk about the power numbers how
much power does it require to do a race
like this how much power do you need to

and and kind of what are the bottlenecks

over a cobble section just before the

corners so this made it a very very hard
lap and a very hard race and it wasn't
just those tight corners and the and the
cobbles and the super high motivation of
all the riders it was also the the
temperature it was very warm this day it
was an average was about over 30 degrees

Standards and all of this made that out

down and why it was so tough so guys
don't forget drop me a like these videos
take a lot of time so show some
appreciation
by giving me your like on the video and

down below in the comment section I'm

watching this video watching the back

I'm gonna point out the team colors so
you know who to watch for so we are a
red jersey and the black bibs
that's our color it's being attacks
after attacks after attack after attack
this race everybody was very motivated
there's 135 riders on the starting list

so you had to earn some kind of points
to be able to race this race and a

currently Frank is in front of me and
he's pulling us just across the entire

like Frank
behind me is Mela in your D and a lot of
us were in the front a long time of this

go down we had a little quick
conversation with each other it's like
okay this is the moment at some point in

when somebody attacks they actually get

that would
not be again people stopped chasing not

we've got Martin Verdi or D for all

videos.title: THE DUTCH DUNES ''100Km along the Dutch Cycle Path''
videos.description:
''THE DUTCH DUNES''

It was a ride that I had been thinking about for a long time.
It's a 100km route that I often use for training on long rides, and a few months ago on a late summer I thought: '' I have to show everyone what a natural paradise this place is''.
So the route as I mentioned above is 100km long, starting from Rotterdam and making a circular loop along the bicycle path that from Hoek Van Hollan to the highest point in the North is 147km.
As I was saying, after leaving Rotterdam I stopped after about 15 km on the only artificial hill in Rotterdam where I had already made a video 👉 ''EVERESTING-TEST in NETHERLANS (Rotterdam)'' and I did some intervals just to take away the need to climb.
Once I arrived at the beach in Hoek van Holland I took the bike path straight to the the famous Dutch Dunes that I absolutely recommend to see if you pass through Holland.
A beautiful day, which makes me more and more convinced of having made the best choice by coming to live in Netherlands, where safety for cyclists is at the first place.
Of course for some people it may not be like that, but since cycling is my passion it's something I care about.

Enjoy the video and let me know what you would like to see about Netherlands and maybe I'll make a video😜 about it.
Leave a 👍 and get subscribe to the channel🤙.

Social NICSTA
🎬YouTube https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJjDocTpkaMP6ivF8cO4e3w
📸Instagram https://www.instagram.com/nicsta.cc/?hl=en
🚴♂️Strava https://www.strava.com/athletes/23601701

Filming Equipment:
- DJI MAVIC MINI https://www.dji.com/nl/mavic-mini?site=brandsite&from=nav
- GOPro Hero 8 https://gopro.com/en/us/shop/cameras/hero8-black/CHDHX-801-master.html

key_challenges.name: Cobble section before finish
key_challenges.description: 17 times over a cobble section just before the finish line makes this very challenging.

key_challenges.name: Sharp corners
key_challenges.description: 17 times through all these sharp corners on the 10 kilometer loop.

terrain_notes.text: 10 kilometer loop repeated 17 times with cobbles and sharp corners that make it very very hard.

race_day_tips.text: Be super super sharp because at some point it's gonna go down and there's gonna be a breakaway. When attacks start getting gaps instead of being chased immediately, that's when the race breaks apart.
Position yourself well when you feel the pace going up and attacks getting more frequent - riders will get tired and stop chasing as effectively.

additional_quotes.text: It was very warm this day it was over 30 degrees Celsius so that's super hot for Dutch Standards and all of this made that out of a hundred and thirty-five riders only sixty five riders actually finished the race.

rider_intel.search_text: The Race Around Netherlands features a challenging 10 kilometer loop format repeated 17 times for 165 kilometers total. Riders face cobbled sections just before the finish line and numerous sharp corners throughout each lap. The repetitive nature creates significant difficulty, with riders going over the same cobbles and corners 17 times. Hot weather conditions over 30 degrees Celsius add to the challenge, contributing to high attrition rates where only 65 out of 135 starters finish. The race features constant attacks and high pace throughout, with tactical positioning becoming critical when riders begin to tire and stop chasing breaks effectively. The loop format means this will never end in a field sprint, requiring riders to be in breakaways to have winning opportunities.
